content.problemStatement:
NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ are placed in a circle in such a way that for each valid iii, AiA_iAi​ and Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​ are adjacent, and A1A_1A1​ and ANA_NAN​ are also adjacent.

We want to repeat the following operation exactly N−1N-1N−1 times (until only one number remains):


Select two adjacent numbers. Let's denote them by aaa and bbb.
Score a+ba+ba+b penalty points.
Erase both aaa and bbb from the circle and insert a+ba+ba+b in the space between them.
What is the minimum number of penalty points we can score?
constraints: a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​ are distinct powers of 222 (including 111)
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of penalty points.
